summary: Define an export/attribution contract so external products can consume Aura Knowledge articles without the organization owning derivative formats.
current_state: Aura Knowledge articles are published primarily for human readers on the garden site. There is no stable machine-readable contract or policy for external reuse. Recent discussions about Medium syndication, Substack newsletters, AI-narrated podcasts, translations, and video markup are drifting toward being treated as internal organizational capabilities rather than separate products. This risks scope creep and entangles knowledge curation with media production.
proposed_change: Document a structured export interface, attribution rules, freshness/lifecycle expectations, and a clear responsibility boundary: Aura Knowledge owns the source article; external products consume it. The existing audio-first-voice-consumption proposal should be treated as the first test case for an external consumer. Derivative-format work (text syndication, podcast, translation, video) should happen outside the core organization unless explicitly adopted as a core workflow after experimentation.
impact: Keeps Aura Knowledge focused on its core mission: credible, sourced, durable knowledge. Lets derivative-format experiments move faster with their own success metrics and maintenance burden. Prevents media-production scope from slowing garden curation. Makes Aura Knowledge more agent-readable by design, which is a separate strategic benefit.
agent_involvement: Drafted by an AI agent during a critical ideation session with the proposer; the proposer reviewed and approved the framing.
